citations.description: list_directory on repo root shows no 'artifacts' directory; search_files for 'jira-cleanup' returns no matches; EXECUTE IN PROGRESS comment says 'Script: $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/jira_comment_cleanup.py'
search_files for 'jira_comment_cleanup' returns no matches in the repository

findings.criterion: Backup artifact exists and passes its per-ticket completeness check — `len(fetched)` from the paginated `get_comments` equals `total` read from the dedicated `GET /rest/api/3/issue/{key}/comment?maxResults=0` envelope — BEFORE any deletion.
findings.detail: No `artifacts/jira-cleanup/` directory exists in the repository, and the cleanup script `jira_comment_cleanup.py` is referenced as living in `$CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/` (a temporary job directory outside the repo). The backup artifact and proving command cannot be verified in the codebase.

citations.description: search_files for 'deletion_filter', 'dedup_planner', 'keep_oldest', 'delete_list' in tests/ all return no matches
search_files for 'comment_cleanup' and 'jira_cleanup' in tests/ return no matches

findings.criterion: Deletion filter provably respects: (a) marked-only (RECONCILER_MARKER present), (b) keep-oldest-per-distinct-body, (c) never single-copy, (d) never unmarked/human. A unit test over a synthetic comment set asserts the delete-list the planner computes matches (a)-(d); RED if the filter mislabels a human/unmarked or single-copy comment as deletable.
findings.detail: No unit test for the cleanup deletion filter exists anywhere in tests/. Searches for 'deletion_filter', 'dedup_planner', 'keep_oldest', 'marked_comment' (in cleanup context), and 'delete_list' all return no matches for a cleanup-specific test. The existing RECONCILER_MARKER tests are for the reconciler itself, not for a cleanup script filter.

citations.description: search_files for 'test_rate_limit' specific to cleanup returns no matches; the cleanup script lives only in $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/
Existing _call_with_retry 429 test covers the reconciler applier, not the cleanup script

findings.criterion: Rate-limit safety: the DELETE loop routes through `dispatch_one._call_with_retry` (the single chosen 429/Retry-After route); a unit test asserts a 429 is retried (not dropped) and `Retry-After` is honored.
findings.detail: No unit test specific to the cleanup script's DELETE loop routing through `_call_with_retry` exists. The existing `test_retry_httperror.py` and `test_applier_retry.py` tests cover the reconciler's own use of `_call_with_retry`, not the cleanup script. The cleanup script itself is not in the repository.

citations.description: search_files for 'maxResults=0' in tests/ returns no matches; search_files for 'completeness_gate' returns no matches

findings.criterion: Completeness-gate enforcement: a unit test asserts the script REFUSES to proceed (non-zero exit, no DELETE issued) when the paginated fetched-count != the `?maxResults=0` reported `total` for any ticket (guards a silently-truncated fetch).
findings.detail: No unit test for this completeness gate exists in the repository. Searches for 'completeness_gate', 'maxResults=0', and 'fetched_count' in tests/ all return no matches.

citations.description: search_files for 'accountId cross-check', 'bot_account', 'diverges' in tests/ context of cleanup returns no matches; cleanup script is in $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/

findings.criterion: accountId cross-check: a unit test asserts (a) a marked comment whose `author.accountId` diverges from the single resolved bot accountId ABORTS the run for manual review (no DELETE), and (b) a homogeneous marked set records the bot accountId and proceeds.
findings.detail: No unit test for the cleanup script's accountId cross-check logic exists in the repository. The cleanup script itself is not in the repository.

citations.description: search_files for 'approval_gate' returns no matches; the dry_run tests found in tests/ all relate to the reconciler workflow, not the cleanup script

findings.criterion: Approval-gate enforcement: a unit/integration test asserts `--dry-run` (default) issues ZERO DELETE calls, and that `--execute` is the ONLY path that issues DELETEs; on operator reject/abort no DELETE is issued and artifacts are retained.
findings.detail: No unit or integration test for the cleanup script's approval gate exists in the repository. Searches for 'approval_gate', 'dry_run' in cleanup context, and 'execute' in cleanup context return no matches.

citations.description: EXECUTE COMPLETE comment: 'the live reconciler (not quiesced during the ~1hr run) re-synced ~12 comments my _normalize_comment_body collapsed more aggressively' — explicit confirmation that quiescing did NOT occur
No attestation of a paused pass cadence or coordinated window between passes is recorded in the ticket

findings.criterion: Reconciler quiescing during `--execute`: the run window is coordinated so the live reconciler does not write to the 13 tickets mid-deletion (either the pass cadence is paused, or the window is chosen between passes). [operator-attested]
findings.detail: The EXECUTE COMPLETE comment explicitly notes 'the live reconciler (not quiesced during the ~1hr run) re-synced ~12 comments' — admitting the reconciler was NOT quiesced. The attestation contains no specific reference id, timestamp, or evidence of coordination. The criterion requires coordination; the attestation confirms non-coordination occurred.
findings.remediation: Record a ticket comment naming: the specific reconcile-bridge run ids that were paused or the specific time window chosen between passes; the actor who coordinated the pause; and confirmation that no reconciler writes occurred to any of the 13 tickets during the deletion window.

citations.description: list_directory on repo root shows no 'artifacts' directory; search_files for 'deletions-run.jsonl' returns no matches
EXECUTE IN PROGRESS comment: 'log at jira-cleanup/deletions-run.jsonl' (in $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp, not artifacts/jira-cleanup/)

findings.criterion: The deletion-log artifact `artifacts/jira-cleanup/deletions-<ts>.jsonl` (one JSON object per line: ticket, comment id, normalized-body hash, outcome) exists for the executed run. Proving command: `wc -l artifacts/jira-cleanup/deletions-<ts>.jsonl` matches the reported delete count.
findings.detail: No `artifacts/jira-cleanup/` directory or `deletions-*.jsonl` file exists in the repository. The ticket comments reference the file as `$CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp/jira-cleanup/deletions-run.jsonl` — a temporary path outside the repository, not in `artifacts/jira-cleanup/` as specified.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
